title: Gas hydrate and free gas estimation from seismic analysis offshore Chiloé island (Chile)
abstract: One seismic section offshore Chiloé Island was analyzed to better define the seismic character of the hydrate-bearing sediments. Velocity analysis was used to estimate the gas-phase concentration and relate it to geological features. The velocity model allowed recognition of two important layers that characterize hydrate- and free gas-bearing sediments above and below the BSR respectively. The velocity above the BSR is high (1,800–2,200 m/s) indicating gas hydrate presence, while below the BSR velocities are low (1,600–1,700 m/s) indicating free gas. Calculated concentrations are 9.5% gas hydrate and 0.5% free gas by volume at maximum. Variations in BSR depth are attributed to geothermal gradients ranging from 25 to 45°C/km.

location.description: Offshore Chiloé Island at ~42°S, southern Chilean margin, gas hydrate province

data.name: Multichannel seismic profile offshore Chiloé Island
data.repository: OGS (Istituto Nazionale di Oceanografia e di Geofisica Sperimentale) or SHOA
data.description: Single MCS reflection section offshore Chiloé Island analyzed for BSR identification and gas hydrate estimation. Velocity model derived for hydrate and free gas quantification.

processing.summary: A single multichannel seismic reflection profile offshore Chiloé Island (~42°S) was processed and analyzed for BSR characterization and gas hydrate/free gas estimation. Standard MCS processing applied. Velocity analysis performed to derive interval velocities for gas hydrate concentration estimation using rock physics relationships. Two-layer velocity model identified: high velocity (1,800–2,200 m/s) above BSR (hydrate-bearing) and low velocity (1,600–1,700 m/s) below BSR (free gas zone).
workflow: 7. Gas hydrate and free gas concentration estimation (rock physics)
8. Geothermal gradient estimation from BSR depth
processing.notes: Gold open access paper in Andean Geology (Chilean national journal). OGS Trieste (Italy) co-authorship suggests Italian-Chilean collaborative dataset.

analysis_notes: Paper is gold open access in Andean Geology with CC-BY license. Full PDF available at journal website. Abstract confirmed from Semantic Scholar. No explicit data repository link found in metadata. Seismic data likely from a prior SHOA or OGS cruise; specific vessel not confirmed.
